w:r.w:t: "Resume Job-Fit Assistant" is a Chrome browser extension designed to serve as a seamless acquisition channel for a resume tool website. When users browse job postings on major recruitment platforms, the extension automatically detects the job content and surfaces a non-intrusive sidebar offering a one-click bridge to the website, carrying structured job data into the resume editing workflow.
The extension contains zero AI functionality and zero API keys. All intelligence lives on the website. The extension's sole job is detection, extraction, and hand-off.
User is browsing a job posting → Extension appears → One click → User lands on your site with job data pre-filled → Higher conversion to paid plan
The extension is a distribution moat: it inserts your website into the user's job-hunting workflow at the highest-intent moment — right when they are reading a job description.
English-speaking job seekers actively applying for jobs, aged 22–45, using Chrome on desktop
Spends 20–60 minutes per session browsing job boards, opens 5–20 job listings per session
Manually copying job requirements into a resume editor is slow and error-prone
Extension promises to speed up tailoring resumes to specific jobs
Free extension → lands on website → upgrades to paid resume builder plan
No AI text generation inside the extension
No storage of resumes or user data inside the extension
No login or authentication inside the extension
No backend server required by the extension itself
No support for Firefox, Safari, or Edge in v1.0
Step 1 → User installs extension from Chrome Web Store
Step 2 → User visits a job posting page (LinkedIn / Indeed / Glassdoor / Lever / Greenhouse)
Step 3 → Extension detects the page is a job posting and automatically extracts: Job Title, Company Name, Location, Job Description text
Step 4 → A compact sidebar badge / icon appears at the edge of the browser window (non-blocking)
Step 5 → User clicks the badge or extension popup
Step 6 → A panel shows a preview of extracted data + CTA button: "Tailor My Resume for This Job"
Step 7 → Clicking the CTA opens your website in a new tab, with job data encoded in the URL
Step 8 → Your website reads the URL parameters and pre-fills the job description field → user begins resume tailoring
Show a manual input field in the popup so the user can paste the job title/description themselves before clicking CTA
Show what was extracted with editable fields; let user correct before sending to website
Extension icon stays inactive (grayed out); no sidebar badge shown
Badge disappears for that tab session; user can still click extension icon in toolbar
Show fallback message: "Our site is temporarily unavailable. Your job data is saved — try again shortly." Store data in chrome.storage.session
As a job seeker, I want the extension to automatically detect I am on a job page so I don't have to do anything manually
As a job seeker, I want to see a summary of the extracted job data before clicking through so I can verify accuracy
As a job seeker, I want one click to open the resume website with the job data pre-loaded
As a job seeker, I want to edit extracted data before sending if it is wrong
As a job seeker, I want the extension to not slow down or clutter my browser
As a job seeker, I want the extension to work on LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor, Lever, and Greenhouse
As a job seeker, I want to save a job post for later from the popup
As a job seeker, I want to see how many jobs I have tailored resumes for
The exact job title as displayed on the page (e.g., "Senior Product Manager")
Full job description text, stripped of HTML tags
Bullet-point requirements if separately marked up in DOM
Salary info if present on page

Each supported platform requires a platform-specific content script. Detection must use DOM selectors rather than URL-only matching to ensure the job data is actually rendered on page before extraction runs. Extraction must fire after the DOM is fully loaded (use MutationObserver for dynamic SPA pages like LinkedIn).
LinkedIn is a heavy React SPA. The URL changes via pushState without full page reloads.
The content script must listen to chrome.webNavigation.onHistoryStateUpdated OR use a MutationObserver on the job detail panel container to re-run extraction when the job detail panel updates.
Do NOT rely on DOMContentLoaded alone on LinkedIn.
Icon states: Active (color) when on a detected job page; Inactive (grayscale) otherwise
Badge text: Show "NEW" on first detection per tab; clear after popup is opened
Clicking the icon always opens the Popup Panel
Dimensions: 360px wide × auto height (max 520px with scroll). Opens anchored to the extension icon in the Chrome toolbar.
Popup panel must contain the following sections:
Extension logo + name. Small "Settings" gear icon (top-right).
Shows: Job Title (large, bold), Company Name, Location. Collapsible section for full Job Description (first 200 chars visible, expand button for more).
"Edit" button that makes all fields editable inline (input/textarea). Allows user to fix extraction errors.
Large button: "Tailor My Resume for This Job →". Opens website in new tab with data in URL params. Button must be visually prominent (brand color, full width).
"Save Job" (P2): stores job to local list. "Copy JD" button: copies job description to clipboard.
If no job detected: show message "Open a job posting on LinkedIn, Indeed, or Glassdoor to get started." with link to supported sites.
Small text: "Powered by [YourSiteName]" with link to website homepage.

A small floating button (40×40px) on the right edge of job pages. Shows extension logo. On hover: expands to show "Tailor Resume →". On click: opens popup OR directly opens website in new tab (configurable in settings).
Must not obstruct Apply button or other key page elements. Default position: right edge, vertically centered. Draggable by user. Position persists in chrome.storage.local.
When the CTA is clicked, the extension opens the website with the following URL structure:
job_description_raw must be Base64-encoded before URL embedding to handle special characters, newlines, and HTML entities.
Max JD length for URL: 8000 characters (after Base64). If longer, truncate to 8000 chars and append &jd_truncated=true
Website must handle both encoded and plain-text values gracefully.
3.3.2 Website Landing Page Behavior (Contract with Website Dev)
This section defines what the website must implement to complete the hand-off:
Route /tailor must be a dedicated landing page for extension traffic
On page load, read and decode all URL parameters listed above
Pre-fill Job Title field, Company field, and Job Description textarea with decoded values
Show a banner: "Job imported from [Platform] via Resume Assistant Extension"
Track the source=extension parameter in analytics (Google Analytics / Plausible)
If user is not logged in: show registration/login modal before allowing edits
Editable field for destination URL (allows white-labeling / staging env switching). Default: production URL.
Toggle: Show / Hide the floating sidebar badge on job pages.
Toggle: Automatically open popup when a job page is detected (default: OFF).
Radio: "Open Popup" vs "Go Directly to Website" (default: Open Popup).
List of saved jobs (P2). Each row shows Job Title + Company + Date + "Open" button.
Button to clear all locally stored data (saved jobs, position prefs).

resume-extension/ ├── manifest.json ← Manifest V3 config ├── background/ │ └── service-worker.js ← Handles tab events, webNavigation ├── content-scripts/ │ ├── detector.js ← Shared detection logic │ ├── linkedin.js ← LinkedIn-specific selectors │ ├── indeed.js ← Indeed-specific selectors │ ├── glassdoor.js ← Glassdoor-specific selectors │ ├── lever.js ← Lever-specific selectors │ └── greenhouse.js ← Greenhouse-specific selectors ├── popup/ │ ├── popup.html │ ├── popup.js │ └── popup.css ├── sidebar/ │ ├── sidebar.js ← Injected floating badge │ └── sidebar.css ├── settings/ │ ├── settings.html │ ├── settings.js │ └── settings.css └── assets/ ├── icon-16.png ├── icon-48.png └── icon-128.png
Read current tab URL and inject content scripts on demand
chrome.storage.local for settings + saved jobs; chrome.storage.session for temp job data
Service workers replace background pages in MV3. Service workers are event-driven and can be terminated at any time.
Do NOT store state in service worker global variables — always use chrome.storage.
chrome.storage.session is cleared when browser closes; use chrome.storage.local for persistence.
Avoid requesting broad host_permissions like <all_urls> — Chrome Web Store may flag it. List exact domains only.

User opens job page ↓ Content Script (platform-specific) fires ↓ Extracts DOM data → {job_title, company, location, jd_raw, job_url} ↓ Sends message to Service Worker: chrome.runtime.sendMessage({type:'JOB_DETECTED', data}) ↓ Service Worker stores data: chrome.storage.session.set({currentJob: data}) Service Worker updates icon badge: chrome.action.setBadgeText({text:'NEW'}) ↓ User clicks extension icon → Popup opens ↓ Popup reads from chrome.storage.session → Renders job preview ↓ User clicks CTA → popup.js constructs URL with params → chrome.tabs.create({url}) ↓ Website opens in new tab → reads URL params → pre-fills form
Each platform content script must export a single async function: extractJobData() → JobData | null
The shared detector.js calls each platform script and aggregates results. If extraction returns null (not a job page), no UI is shown.
These selectors are valid as of document writing date. Job board DOM structures change frequently.
Build a selector configuration object (not hardcoded) so selectors can be updated without code changes.
Consider a remote selector config hosted on your website (fetched once daily) to allow hotfixes without extension updates.
Infer from page title or og:site_name meta tag
Minimal and clean — the extension should feel like a native browser feature, not an ad
Brand consistent with your main website — use same primary color, font family, and logo
Fast — popup must render within 150ms of opening (all data pre-cached in storage)
Non-intrusive — never block job page content. Sidebar badge is dismissible.

┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [LOGO] Resume Assistant [⚙ ] │ ← Header (brand color bg) ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ ✅ Job detected on LinkedIn │ ← Status indicator ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Senior Product Manager │ ← Job Title (large, bold) │ Acme Corporation · San Francisco, CA │ ← Company · Location │ │ │ Job Description: │ │ We are looking for a Senior PM to... │ ← First 200 chars │ [Show full description ▼] │ ← Expand toggle │ │ │ [✎ Edit fields] │ ← Edit toggle ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ ██████████████████████████████████████ │ │ █ Tailor My Resume for This Job → █ │ ← Primary CTA (full width) │ ██████████████████████████████████████ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ [📋 Copy JD] [🔖 Save Job] │ ← Secondary actions ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ Powered by YourSiteName.com │ ← Footer └─────────────────────────────────────────┘

Default (collapsed): On hover (expanded):
╔══╗ ╔═══════════════════╗ ║ R ║ ║ R Tailor Resume →║ ╚══╝ ╚═══════════════════╝
Positioned at right edge of viewport, vertically centered. 40×40px collapsed, expands to ~200px wide on hover.
Extension is on a recognized job page with successfully extracted data
Extraction in progress (for slow-loading pages). Show for max 3 seconds.
Partial extraction — some fields missing, user action may be needed
Not on a job page, or page not supported
Content script injection: < 50ms after DOM ready
Extension must not cause measurable performance degradation on host pages (< 5ms added page interaction latency)
Service worker must not persist unnecessary listeners that drain battery
Privacy Commitment — Critical for Chrome Web Store Approval
The extension does NOT collect, store, or transmit any personal user data to external servers.
All data (extracted job fields, settings, saved jobs) is stored exclusively in chrome.storage.local on the user's device.
The only outbound network request is the user-initiated CTA click opening your website — no background pings, no analytics calls, no telemetry from the extension itself.
This must be clearly stated in: (1) Chrome Web Store listing, (2) Privacy Policy, (3) Extension description.
No cross-origin fetch requests from content scripts
No eval() or dynamic code execution
Content Security Policy headers must be set in manifest.json
Extension must pass Chrome Web Store automated security review
If any content script throws an error, it must fail silently — never show JS errors or break the host page
Extraction failures must be caught and result in the 'partial/manual' UI state, not a broken popup
Service worker crash must not break extension — should auto-restart via event listeners
Chrome version: 88+ (Manifest V3 minimum supported version)
Must work correctly when LinkedIn/Indeed are loaded in: normal tab, pinned tab, incognito (with user permission)
Auto-detect job postings on LinkedIn & Indeed. One click to tailor your resume for any job. No AI needed.
Screenshots: 1280×800px or 640×400px, minimum 1 required, up to 5 recommended
Privacy Policy URL: required (host on your website)
What data the extension accesses (job page DOM content, temporarily)
What data is stored (settings and saved jobs in chrome.storage.local only)
Explicit statement: "We do not collect, sell, or share any personal data"

Chrome Web Store review typically takes 1–3 business days for new submissions. Extensions requesting host_permissions for specific domains (vs. <all_urls>) are reviewed faster. Expect 1 revision cycle.
manifest.json, LinkedIn content script, basic popup (no sidebar badge), CTA opens website with URL params
Add Indeed, Glassdoor, Lever, Greenhouse content scripts. SPA navigation fix for LinkedIn. Partial extraction UI.
Sidebar badge, icon states (active/inactive), Settings page, Edit fields feature, Copy JD button
Cross-platform testing, CSP hardening, privacy review, Chrome Web Store submission, respond to review feedback
Saved jobs feature, remote selector config, usage analytics on website side
Feature works on all 5 supported platforms
Fails gracefully (no errors in console on unsupported pages)
Works after Chrome restarts (service worker re-registration)
Open LinkedIn job posting → check icon state
Click extension icon on LinkedIn job page
Popup shows correct job title, company, location
New tab opens with website URL containing all params
Job title and JD are pre-filled on /tailor page
Navigate LinkedIn feed (not a job page)
Icon is grayscale, popup shows 'no job detected' state
Click job listing on LinkedIn (SPA navigation)
Extension re-detects and updates data for new job
JD is truncated, jd_truncated=true param present
Characters correctly Base64 encoded and decoded on website
Edit fields in popup before clicking CTA
Edited values (not original) are sent to website
Badge disappears for that tab session
Works correctly if user has granted incognito permission
Settings persist; session job data is cleared
Should the sidebar badge be enabled by default (ON) or require user to enable in settings (OFF)? Recommendation: ON by default — higher engagement, user can disable.
Should 'Save Job' (P2) store data indefinitely or with a 30-day auto-expiry? Recommendation: 30-day expiry to limit storage bloat.
Remote selector config: host on your own domain or use a GitHub raw file? Recommendation: your own domain — avoids GitHub dependency and allows auth.
Should the extension track conversion events? If yes, fire a ping to your analytics endpoint (no PII). Decision needed before Phase 4.
What should the extension name be on the Chrome Web Store? Finalize before Phase 4 submission.
PRD: Resume Job-Fit Assistant Chrome Extension | v1.0
